- Maintain the 'Restro Style' visual palette, layout, and hierarchy.
- Remove all mentions of "climatización", "A/C", "temperatura", and "confort".
- Replace the "CLIMATIZACIÓN" card (currently showing 22°C) with a "ESTADO ENERGÉTICO" or "ESTADO DEL CONTRATO" card.
- In the "Consumo Actual" card, ensure it refers to "Consumo global" or "Carga actual" rather than just A/C.
- In the central recommendation block, replace "A/C activo sin reserva detectado" with "Consumo anómalo detectado (fuera de reserva)". Update the recommendation text to focus on checking assets and resolving consumption anomalies generally.
- Change buttons like "Apagar clima" to "Validar consumo" or "Resolver anomalía".
- In the "Historial de Eventos", replace technical A/C events with broader energy management events: "Consumo elevado detectado", "Check-out verificado", "Potencia contratada revisada", "Informe mensual generado".
- In the "Optimización Tarifa y Potencia" section, keep the focus on cost savings and demand peaks, ensuring it feels like a B2B SaaS tool.
- Ensure all text is in professional Spanish.

## Brand & Style

The design system is centered on a "Restro" aesthetic—a sophisticated blend of mid-century modularity and contemporary precision. It targets high-level energy operators who require clarity and calm in complex data environments. The brand personality is professional and architectural, eschewing digital trends like glassmorphism or neon accents in favor of a timeless, tactile editorial feel. 

The style is defined by a warm, paper-like foundation, structured through precise geometric containers and generous negative space. It evokes a sense of "operational luxury," where the premium quality is derived from perfect alignment, subtle tonal shifts, and high-contrast typography rather than decorative effects.

## Colors

The color palette is grounded in warm, organic neutrals to reduce eye strain and provide a "stable" canvas for energy data. 

- **Primary & Secondary:** Caramel and warm orange are used sparingly as functional accents for critical actions, active states, and specific data points.
- **Neutrals:** The background uses a sequence of warm grays (#F4F1EA) to create depth without relying on shadows. Text is kept at a high-contrast near-black (#171717) for absolute legibility.
- **Surfaces:** Pure white and very light cream are used to distinguish modular "cards" from the page background.
- **Semantic Colors:** While not part of the core brand, status indicators (Success/Warning/Error) should be desaturated to maintain the sober, professional tone.

## Typography

This design system utilizes **Plus Jakarta Sans** for its balanced, modern geometric proportions that remain legible at small sizes. The hierarchy is sober and data-centric.

- **Numbers:** Data values are prioritized with larger font sizes and medium weights to ensure they are the first thing an operator sees.
- **Labels:** Small labels use a semi-bold weight and slight letter spacing for clarity in compact sidebar or chart environments.
- **Headlines:** Titles are kept to a medium weight; bolding is reserved for critical alerts or buttons.
- **Scaling:** On mobile devices, `display-lg` should scale down to `32px` to maintain layout integrity.

## Layout & Spacing

The layout follows a **modular fixed-grid** philosophy. Content is organized into distinct cards that act as independent modules within a flexible 12-column grid.

- **Rhythm:** A 4px baseline grid ensures consistent vertical rhythm.
- **Airiness:** Large internal padding (32px) within containers prevents data density from feeling overwhelming.
- **Sidebars:** The primary navigation is a narrow (72px - 80px) vertical bar with circular icon buttons, maximizing horizontal space for data visualization.
- **Breakpoints:** 
    - **Desktop (1440px+):** 12 columns, 40px margins.
    - **Tablet (768px - 1024px):** 6 columns, 24px margins, sidebars may collapse to icons-only.
    - **Mobile (<768px):** Single column fluid, 16px margins, cards stack vertically.

## Elevation & Depth

This design system avoids heavy drop shadows and 3D effects. Depth is communicated through:

- **Tonal Layering:** Main surfaces (#FFFFFF) sit on top of background layers (#F4F1EA).
- **Subtle Borders:** All main containers use a fine 1px border (#E8E2D8).
- **Micro-Shadows:** Only the top-level active cards or dropdowns utilize a very soft, highly diffused shadow (e.g., `0 4px 20px rgba(23, 23, 23, 0.03)`).
- **Interaction:** Hover states are indicated by subtle background shifts (from White to Light Cream) or a primary-colored border stroke rather than an increase in elevation.

## Shapes

The shape language is defined by "Oversized Softness." 

- **Main Containers:** Modular cards use a radius of 24px to 32px (`rounded-xl` or custom), creating a friendly but structured appearance.
- **UI Elements:** Buttons and input fields use a consistent 8px to 12px radius. 
- **Circular Elements:** Sidebar icons and status dots are always fully rounded (pills) to provide a geometric counterpoint to the large rectangular cards.
- **Charts:** Bar charts must have rounded caps; line charts should use a slight curve (interpolation) to match the overall softness of the system.

## Components

- **Buttons:** Primary buttons are solid caramel (#E6813A) with white text. Secondary buttons use a cream background with a subtle border. All buttons are compact with generous horizontal padding.
- **Cards:** The core of the system. Every card has a 1px #E8E2D8 border, 32px corner radius, and white background. Title areas in cards are clearly separated by whitespace rather than lines.
- **Inputs:** Clean, 1px bordered fields. Focus states use a caramel border ring. Labels sit above the field in `label-caps` style.
- **Pills/Chips:** Used in the topbar for status and filtering. These are small, fully rounded elements with light-gray backgrounds or primary-tinted backgrounds for active states.
- **Iconography:** Use 2px stroke-width linear icons. Icons should be strictly geometric and never filled unless they represent an active state.
- **Data Visuals:** Fine-line charts with no area fills. ## Brand & Style

The design system is centered on a "Restro" aesthetic—a sophisticated blend of mid-century modularity and contemporary precision. It targets high-level energy operators who require clarity and calm in complex data environments. The brand personality is professional and architectural, eschewing digital trends like glassmorphism or neon accents in favor of a timeless, tactile editorial feel. 

The style is defined by a warm, paper-like foundation, structured through precise geometric containers and generous negative space. It evokes a sense of "operational luxury," where the premium quality is derived from perfect alignment, subtle tonal shifts, and high-contrast typography rather than decorative effects.

## Colors

The color palette is grounded in warm, organic neutrals to reduce eye strain and provide a "stable" canvas for energy data. 

- **Primary & Secondary:** Caramel and warm orange are used sparingly as functional accents for critical actions, active states, and specific data points.
- **Neutrals:** The background uses a sequence of warm grays (#F4F1EA) to create depth without relying on shadows. Text is kept at a high-contrast near-black (#171717) for absolute legibility.
- **Surfaces:** Pure white and very light cream are used to distinguish modular "cards" from the page background.
- **Semantic Colors:** While not part of the core brand, status indicators (Success/Warning/Error) should be desaturated to maintain the sober, professional tone.

## Typography

This design system utilizes **Plus Jakarta Sans** for its balanced, modern geometric proportions that remain legible at small sizes. The hierarchy is sober and data-centric.

- **Numbers:** Data values are prioritized with larger font sizes and medium weights to ensure they are the first thing an operator sees.
- **Labels:** Small labels use a semi-bold weight and slight letter spacing for clarity in compact sidebar or chart environments.
- **Headlines:** Titles are kept to a medium weight; bolding is reserved for critical alerts or buttons.
- **Scaling:** On mobile devices, `display-lg` should scale down to `32px` to maintain layout integrity.

## Layout & Spacing

The layout follows a **modular fixed-grid** philosophy. Content is organized into distinct cards that act as independent modules within a flexible 12-column grid.

- **Rhythm:** A 4px baseline grid ensures consistent vertical rhythm.
- **Airiness:** Large internal padding (32px) within containers prevents data density from feeling overwhelming.
- **Sidebars:** The primary navigation is a narrow (72px - 80px) vertical bar with circular icon buttons, maximizing horizontal space for data visualization.
- **Breakpoints:** 
    - **Desktop (1440px+):** 12 columns, 40px margins.
    - **Tablet (768px - 1024px):** 6 columns, 24px margins, sidebars may collapse to icons-only.
    - **Mobile (<768px):** Single column fluid, 16px margins, cards stack vertically.

## Elevation & Depth

This design system avoids heavy drop shadows and 3D effects. Depth is communicated through:

- **Tonal Layering:** Main surfaces (#FFFFFF) sit on top of background layers (#F4F1EA).
- **Subtle Borders:** All main containers use a fine 1px border (#E8E2D8).
- **Micro-Shadows:** Only the top-level active cards or dropdowns utilize a very soft, highly diffused shadow (e.g., `0 4px 20px rgba(23, 23, 23, 0.03)`).
- **Interaction:** Hover states are indicated by subtle background shifts (from White to Light Cream) or a primary-colored border stroke rather than an increase in elevation.

## Shapes

The shape language is defined by "Oversized Softness." 

- **Main Containers:** Modular cards use a radius of 24px to 32px (`rounded-xl` or custom), creating a friendly but structured appearance.
- **UI Elements:** Buttons and input fields use a consistent 8px to 12px radius. 
- **Circular Elements:** Sidebar icons and status dots are always fully rounded (pills) to provide a geometric counterpoint to the large rectangular cards.
- **Charts:** Bar charts must have rounded caps; line charts should use a slight curve (interpolation) to match the overall softness of the system.

## Components

- **Buttons:** Primary buttons are solid caramel (#E6813A) with white text. Secondary buttons use a cream background with a subtle border. All buttons are compact with generous horizontal padding.
- **Cards:** The core of the system. Every card has a 1px #E8E2D8 border, 32px corner radius, and white background. Title areas in cards are clearly separated by whitespace rather than lines.
- **Inputs:** Clean, 1px bordered fields. Focus states use a caramel border ring. Labels sit above the field in `label-caps` style.
- **Pills/Chips:** Used in the topbar for status and filtering. These are small, fully rounded elements with light-gray backgrounds or primary-tinted backgrounds for active states.
- **Iconography:** Use 2px stroke-width linear icons. Icons should be strictly geometric and never filled unless they represent an active state.
- **Data Visuals:** Fine-line charts with no area fills. Use the caramel accent for the primary data series and neutral grays for secondary comparisons.","colorVariant":"FIDELITY","overridePrimaryColor":"#e6813a","overrideSecondaryColor":"#d97706","overrideNeutralColor":"#171717","spacingScale":2,"typography":{"body-md":{"fontFamily":"Plus Jakarta Sans","fontSize":"16px","fontWeight":"400","lineHeight":"24px"},"display-lg":{"fontFamily":"Plus Jakarta Sans","fontSize":"48px","fontWeight":"600","lineHeight":"56px","letterSpacing":"-0.02em"},"headline-md":{"fontFamily":"Plus Jakarta Sans","fontSize":"24px","fontWeight":"500","lineHeight":"32px","letterSpacing":"-0.01em"},"label-caps":{"fontFamily":"Plus Jakarta Sans","fontSize":"12px","fontWeight":"600","lineHeight":"16px","letterSpacing":"0.05em"},"number-data":{"fontFamily":"Plus Jakarta Sans","fontSize":"32px","fontWeight":"500","lineHeight":"40px","letterSpacing":"-0.02em"},"title-sm":{"fontFamily":"Plus Jakarta Sans","fontSize":"18px","fontWeight":"500","lineHeight":"24px"}},"spacing":{"container-padding":"32px","gutter":"24px","margin-desktop":"40px","margin-mobile":"16px","unit":"4px"},"headlineFontFamily":"Plus Jakarta Sans","bodyFontFamily":"Plus Jakarta Sans","labelFontFamily":"Plus Jakarta Sans"}},"version":"1"}}],"theme":{}}},{"text":"He transformado la pantalla de **Detalle de Apartamento** para consolidar la visión de EnergyOS como un sistema operativo energético integral, eliminando el enfoque exclusivo en climatización y control térmico.

### Evolución del activo energético:
- **Gestión Integral de Carga:** He sustituido el panel de climatización por un indicador de **Estado Energético** global. Ahora la pantalla narra la historia operativa completa: desde el estado del suministro hasta la carga actual alineada con el calendario de reservas.
- **Resolución de Anomalías:** La alerta principal se ha redefinido como **"Consumo anómalo detectado (fuera de reserva)"**, ofreciendo un flujo de validación de activos en lugar de un simple apagado de A/C.
- **Historial de Eventos Operativos:** He actualizado el log para reflejar hitos de gestión real: revisiones de potencia contratada, detección de consumos elevados y generación automática de informes para propietarios.
- **Contexto de Tarifa y Potencia:** He mantenido y refinado la sección de optimización para asegurar que la gestora tenga visibilidad sobre el ahorro potencial y los picos de demanda, reforzando el valor de la plataforma como herramienta de ahorro preventivo.
- **Fidelidad "Restro":** Se mantiene la coherencia visual absoluta con la paleta crema/naranja y la tipografía Plus Jakarta Sans, asegurando que esta profundidad operativa no sacrifique la elegancia de la interfaz.

¿Deseas que revisemos ahora la pantalla de **Informes Mensuales** o prefieres ajustar algún parámetro de la **Configuración** del sistema?"},{"suggestion":"Ir a Informes Mensuales"},{"suggestion":"Ver Configuración del sistema"},{"suggestion":"Ajustar alertas de potencia"}]}

designSystem.styleGuidelines:
## Brand & Style

The design system is centered on a "Restro" aesthetic—a sophisticated blend of mid-century modularity and contemporary precision. It targets high-level energy operators who require clarity and calm in complex data environments. The brand personality is professional and architectural, eschewing digital trends like glassmorphism or neon accents in favor of a timeless, tactile editorial feel. 

The style is defined by a warm, paper-like foundation, structured through precise geometric containers and generous negative space. It evokes a sense of "operational luxury," where the premium quality is derived from perfect alignment, subtle tonal shifts, and high-contrast typography rather than decorative effects.

## Layout & Spacing

The layout follows a **modular fixed-grid** philosophy. Content is organized into distinct cards that act as independent modules within a flexible 12-column grid.

- **Rhythm:** A 4px baseline grid ensures consistent vertical rhythm.
- **Airiness:** Large internal padding (32px) within containers prevents data density from feeling overwhelming.
- **Sidebars:** The primary navigation is a narrow (72px - 80px) vertical bar with circular icon buttons, maximizing horizontal space for data visualization.
- **Breakpoints:** 
    - **Desktop (1440px+):** 12 columns, 40px margins.
    - **Tablet (768px - 1024px):** 6 columns, 24px margins, sidebars may collapse to icons-only.
    - **Mobile (<768px):** Single column fluid, 16px margins, cards stack vertically.

## Elevation & Depth

This design system avoids heavy drop shadows and 3D effects. Depth is communicated through:

- **Tonal Layering:** Main surfaces (#FFFFFF) sit on top of background layers (#F4F1EA).
- **Subtle Borders:** All main containers use a fine 1px border (#E8E2D8).
- **Micro-Shadows:** Only the top-level active cards or dropdowns utilize a very soft, highly diffused shadow (e.g., `0 4px 20px rgba(23, 23, 23, 0.03)`).
- **Interaction:** Hover states are indicated by subtle background shifts (from White to Light Cream) or a primary-colored border stroke rather than an increase in elevation.

## Components

- **Buttons:** Primary buttons are solid caramel (#E6813A) with white text. Secondary buttons use a cream background with a subtle border. All buttons are compact with generous horizontal padding.
- **Cards:** The core of the system. Every card has a 1px #E8E2D8 border, 32px corner radius, and white background. Title areas in cards are clearly separated by whitespace rather than lines.
- **Inputs:** Clean, 1px bordered fields. Focus states use a caramel border ring. Labels sit above the field in `label-caps` style.
- **Pills/Chips:** Used in the topbar for status and filtering. These are small, fully rounded elements with light-gray backgrounds or primary-tinted backgrounds for active states.
- **Iconography:** Use 2px stroke-width linear icons. Icons should be strictly geometric and never filled unless they represent an active state.
- **Data Visuals:** Fine-line charts with no area fills. Use the caramel accent for the primary data series and neutral grays for secondary comparisons.

name: EnergyOS Restro

  on-tertiary-fixed-variant: '#004e60'

  on-background: '#1c1b1b'

typography:

    fontFamily: Plus Jakarta Sans
    fontSize: 48px
    fontWeight: '600'
    lineHeight: 56px

  headline-md:

## Brand & Style

The design system is centered on a "Restro" aesthetic—a sophisticated blend of mid-century modularity and contemporary precision. It targets high-level energy operators who require clarity and calm in complex data environments. The brand personality is professional and architectural, eschewing digital trends like glassmorphism or neon accents in favor of a timeless, tactile editorial feel. 

The style is defined by a warm, paper-like foundation, structured through precise geometric containers and generous negative space. It evokes a sense of "operational luxury," where the premium quality is derived from perfect alignment, subtle tonal shifts, and high-contrast typography rather than decorative effects.

## Colors

The color palette is grounded in warm, organic neutrals to reduce eye strain and provide a "stable" canvas for energy data. 

- **Primary & Secondary:** Caramel and warm orange are used sparingly as functional accents for critical actions, active states, and specific data points.
- **Neutrals:** The background uses a sequence of warm grays (#F4F1EA) to create depth without relying on shadows. Text is kept at a high-contrast near-black (#171717) for absolute legibility.
- **Surfaces:** Pure white and very light cream are used to distinguish modular "cards" from the page background.
- **Semantic Colors:** While not part of the core brand, status indicators (Success/Warning/Error) should be desaturated to maintain the sober, professional tone.

## Typography

This design system utilizes **Plus Jakarta Sans** for its balanced, modern geometric proportions that remain legible at small sizes. The hierarchy is sober and data-centric.

- **Numbers:** Data values are prioritized with larger font sizes and medium weights to ensure they are the first thing an operator sees.
- **Labels:** Small labels use a semi-bold weight and slight letter spacing for clarity in compact sidebar or chart environments.
- **Headlines:** Titles are kept to a medium weight; bolding is reserved for critical alerts or buttons.
- **Scaling:** On mobile devices, `display-lg` should scale down to `32px` to maintain layout integrity.

## Layout & Spacing

The layout follows a **modular fixed-grid** philosophy. Content is organized into distinct cards that act as independent modules within a flexible 12-column grid.

- **Rhythm:** A 4px baseline grid ensures consistent vertical rhythm.
- **Airiness:** Large internal padding (32px) within containers prevents data density from feeling overwhelming.
- **Sidebars:** The primary navigation is a narrow (72px - 80px) vertical bar with circular icon buttons, maximizing horizontal space for data visualization.
- **Breakpoints:** 
    - **Desktop (1440px+):** 12 columns, 40px margins.
    - **Tablet (768px - 1024px):** 6 columns, 24px margins, sidebars may collapse to icons-only.
    - **Mobile (<768px):** Single column fluid, 16px margins, cards stack vertically.

## Elevation & Depth

This design system avoids heavy drop shadows and 3D effects. Depth is communicated through:

- **Tonal Layering:** Main surfaces (#FFFFFF) sit on top of background layers (#F4F1EA).
- **Subtle Borders:** All main containers use a fine 1px border (#E8E2D8).
- **Micro-Shadows:** Only the top-level active cards or dropdowns utilize a very soft, highly diffused shadow (e.g., `0 4px 20px rgba(23, 23, 23, 0.03)`).
- **Interaction:** Hover states are indicated by subtle background shifts (from White to Light Cream) or a primary-colored border stroke rather than an increase in elevation.

## Shapes

The shape language is defined by "Oversized Softness." 

- **Main Containers:** Modular cards use a radius of 24px to 32px (`rounded-xl` or custom), creating a friendly but structured appearance.
- **UI Elements:** Buttons and input fields use a consistent 8px to 12px radius. 
- **Circular Elements:** Sidebar icons and status dots are always fully rounded (pills) to provide a geometric counterpoint to the large rectangular cards.
- **Charts:** Bar charts must have rounded caps; line charts should use a slight curve (interpolation) to match the overall softness of the system.

## Components

- **Buttons:** Primary buttons are solid caramel (#E6813A) with white text. Secondary buttons use a cream background with a subtle border. All buttons are compact with generous horizontal padding.
- **Cards:** The core of the system. Every card has a 1px #E8E2D8 border, 32px corner radius, and white background. Title areas in cards are clearly separated by whitespace rather than lines.
- **Inputs:** Clean, 1px bordered fields. Focus states use a caramel border ring. Labels sit above the field in `label-caps` style.
- **Pills/Chips:** Used in the topbar for status and filtering. These are small, fully rounded elements with light-gray backgrounds or primary-tinted backgrounds for active states.
- **Iconography:** Use 2px stroke-width linear icons. Icons should be strictly geometric and never filled unless they represent an active state.
- **Data Visuals:** Fine-line charts with no area fills. Use the caramel accent for the primary data series and neutral grays for secondary comparisons.

screens.name: projects/11820050226044719534/screens/76cf6d979c844aafa99138b3940db6e6
screens.prompt:
Update the apartment detail screen to align with the "Energy OS" strategic objective. 
- Maintain the 'Restro Style' visual palette, layout, and hierarchy.
- Remove all mentions of "climatización", "A/C", "temperatura", and "confort".
- Replace the "CLIMATIZACIÓN" card (currently showing 22°C) with a "ESTADO ENERGÉTICO" or "ESTADO DEL CONTRATO" card.
- In the "Consumo Actual" card, ensure it refers to "Consumo global" or "Carga actual" rather than just A/C.
- In the central recommendation block, replace "A/C activo sin reserva detectado" with "Consumo anómalo detectado (fuera de reserva)". Update the recommendation text to focus on checking assets and resolving consumption anomalies generally.
- Change buttons like "Apagar clima" to "Validar consumo" or "Resolver anomalía".
- In the "Historial de Eventos", replace technical A/C events with broader energy management events: "Consumo elevado detectado", "Check-out verificado", "Potencia contratada revisada", "Informe mensual generado".
- In the "Optimización Tarifa y Potencia" section, keep the focus on cost savings and demand peaks, ensuring it feels like a B2B SaaS tool.
- Ensure all text is in professional Spanish.
- Do NOT use the forbidden strings: "climatización", "A/C", "temperatura", "confort", "ROI garantizado", "trading", "48 monitorizados", "7 alertas", "-12%", "3,8 kW".

screens.title: Detalle apartamento — EnergyOS (Restro Style)

outputComponents.text:
He transformado la pantalla de **Detalle de Apartamento** para consolidar la visión de EnergyOS como un sistema operativo energético integral, eliminando el enfoque exclusivo en climatización y control térmico.

### Evolución del activo energético:
- **Gestión Integral de Carga:** He sustituido el panel de climatización por un indicador de **Estado Energético** global. Ahora la pantalla narra la historia operativa completa: desde el estado del suministro hasta la carga actual alineada con el calendario de reservas.
- **Resolución de Anomalías:** La alerta principal se ha redefinido como **"Consumo anómalo detectado (fuera de reserva)"**, ofreciendo un flujo de validación de activos en lugar de un simple apagado de A/C.
- **Historial de Eventos Operativos:** He actualizado el log para reflejar hitos de gestión real: revisiones de potencia contratada, detección de consumos elevados y generación automática de informes para propietarios.
- **Contexto de Tarifa y Potencia:** He mantenido y refinado la sección de optimización para asegurar que la gestora tenga visibilidad sobre el ahorro potencial y los picos de demanda, reforzando el valor de la plataforma como herramienta de ahorro preventivo.
- **Fidelidad "Restro":** Se mantiene la coherencia visual absoluta con la paleta crema/naranja y la tipografía Plus Jakarta Sans, asegurando que esta profundidad operativa no sacrifique la elegancia de la interfaz.

¿Deseas que revisemos ahora la pantalla de **Informes Mensuales** o prefieres ajustar algún parámetro de la **Configuración** del sistema?
